title: Geographic consistency and variation in conflicting selection generated by pollinators and seed predators
metadata_reason_for_priority: Metadata abstract reports floral traits, pollen-load and seed-predation measures across 12–14 populations; strongest apparent direct-regime prospect.
first_full_text_question: Are attraction and floral-barrier traits both measured on the same plant/population units, with pollen and seed-predation denominators recoverable?
do_not_infer: Do not call this D1 from abstract or title.

title: Direct and indirect effects of pollinators and seed predators to selection on plant and floral traits
metadata_reason_for_priority: Metadata abstract explicitly contrasts pollination and pre-dispersal seed predation as selection agents on plant and floral characters.
first_full_text_question: Is there a recoverable plant-level table and a separately defined floral barrier/resistance trait, rather than only floral-attraction traits plus seed damage?
do_not_infer: Do not equate seed predation alone with a measured B_flower trait.

title: Consequences of multiple flower–insect interactions for subsequent plant–insect interactions and plant reproduction
metadata_reason_for_priority: Metadata abstract reports manipulated florivory, nectar robbing and pollination together with attraction traits, floral-defence traits and reproduction.
first_full_text_question: Does the paper provide a plant-level table linking trait changes, pollination response and floral-antagonist response in a common time window?
do_not_infer: Manipulation of interactions is not itself an A_flower × B_flower test.

title: Attracting mutualists and antagonists: Plant trait variation explains the distribution of specialist floral herbivores and pollinators on crops and wild gourds
metadata_reason_for_priority: Metadata abstract states that floral traits, pollinator attraction and herbivore resistance were evaluated across 20 taxa/varieties.
first_full_text_question: Are trait and interaction outcomes linked within the same plant/taxon–site unit, and do the data distinguish floral from leaf herbivory?
do_not_infer: Species-level comparative association is not automatically a D1 regime test.

title: Effects of Floral Traits on Sequential Components of Fitness in Ipomopsis aggregata
metadata_reason_for_priority: Metadata abstract reports individual-plant floral traits, pollen receipt, seed-set stages and undamaged seeds.
first_full_text_question: Is floral antagonism explicitly measured with a denominator, and is any separately justified B_flower trait present?
do_not_infer: Undamaged-seed count does not by itself document floral-antagonist pressure.

title: Pollinators shift to nectar robbers when florivory occurs, with effects on reproductive success in Iris bulleyana
metadata_reason_for_priority: Metadata abstract reports florivory, legitimate visits/robbing and reproductive response in a field manipulation.
first_full_text_question: Which measured intrinsic floral traits can be mapped to A_flower and B_flower, and are responses linked at plant/flower level?
do_not_infer: Flower damage treatment is not an intrinsic barrier trait unless a defensible mapping is stated.
